description: In September 2012, the Chicago Teachers Union (CTU) engaged in a historic seven-day strike against the Chicago Public Schools over contract disputes involving teacher evaluations and school closures. The strike concluded with a contract agreement that was largely viewed as a victory for the union, restoring public support during the walkout.

key_events.description: CTU rejects initial offer and official strike begins.
Massive teacher rally in downtown Chicago shifts public sentiment.
Tentative agreement reached, ending the strike.
Union members ratify the contract by a wide margin.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring how public opinion shifts toward organized labor when the narrative focuses on pedagogical quality rather than just fiscal cost.
